title: Track Field Additions and Removals in Dictionary Field History
story: As a Salesforce administrator using DataTools Pro, I want the Dictionary field history to show when fields are added to or removed from a Salesforce object, so that I can track schema changes over time without checking Salesforce directly.
description:
The Dictionary field history was enhanced to track two additional change types beyond field updates:

1. **New Field Detection**: When a refresh pulls fields from Salesforce that do not exist in the local data model, they are logged as 'New Field' in the history.

2. **Field Removal (Soft Delete)**: When a previously tracked field is no longer returned by the Salesforce describe/SOQL query, it is flagged as deleted in the data model and logged as 'Deleted' in the history. The field is soft-deleted (not physically removed) so the history record is preserved.

Testing was performed by adding and then deleting a field (`Lead.Is_Activated__c`) in Salesforce and refreshing from DataTools Pro.
acceptance_criteria: Refreshing an object detects newly added fields from Salesforce and logs them as 'New Field' in history
Refreshing an object detects removed fields and flags them as soft-deleted with a 'Deleted' history entry
Soft-deleted fields are hidden from the active field list but preserved in history
Field history view shows New Field, Field Updated, and Deleted change types
Deleting a test field from Salesforce and refreshing produces the expected 'Deleted' history entry
ux_changes: Field history view now includes 'New Field' and 'Deleted' change type entries alongside existing 'Field Updated' entries.
middleware_changes: Refresh logic compares the incoming Salesforce field set against the local data model to detect additions and removals. New fields trigger an insert + history log. Missing fields trigger a soft-delete flag + history log.
data_model_changes: Added a soft-delete flag to the fields table. History log entries now include 'New Field' and 'Deleted' change types.
implementation_notes: The 'Field Updated' and 'New Field' history entries were added in a prior sprint. This ticket added the 'Deleted' (soft-delete) capability. Testing used the Lead.Is_Activated__c field as a sacrificial test field. Implementation took longer than expected due to the delete detection logic.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed prior work already tracked 'Field Updated' and 'New Field' history. The remaining piece was soft-delete detection. Developer implemented and tested on production, including the ability to mark fields as deleted when they no longer appear in the Salesforce response.
